The ability to manage your state is very temporary. In other words, even the best of us, you can't keep yourself in an artificially inflated state for more than like 10, 15 minutes at most or in one particular sale. In other words, you pop into a state before you start a sale, and then you can maintain that state while you're in the sale. Once you pop in, if you enter in this peak state, you can ride that out through the entire sale. But once that sales over, whether it's a winner or loss, you will then tend to fall back into the state you were in, especially if it was a loss. In other words, again, you're not going to close everyone. Even in the best of circumstances, when you're really kicking ass, you might close one out of three, two out of three, whatever it might be, doesn't matter. It's enough to get rich with. But the point is that if your last sale was not a winning outcome, you can close the person. Okay, who you are, there's a bit of a dip there. There's a bit of a letdown because you don't get to bask in that afterglow of closing, which is just the greatest feel in the world. In fact, the anchor that I told you guys to set using olfactory anchoring was to wait until the moment you close the sale and then use that as your launch and off point, as your state, you're in that state naturally, organically, and then you set your anchor, right? Why am I telling you this? Reason being is that I want you to remember that state management is not something you just trigger once at the beginning of each day. I want you to think about this in terms of every single sale. Every sale is like a new day. Every pitch is a new outcome. I want you to look at every single pitch you make as the beginning of a new day. That whatever happened beforehand, if it was negative, doesn't matter. And if whatever happened was great, then it doesn't matter. Like you build on your successes, right? So you had a great outcome to the last sale, you ride that afterglow, fire your anchor again, bam, ratchet up to even haul your state of certainty now, get in a roll. And if your outcome was negative, you shake it off, refire your anchor, give it a certainty and bam, you keep going. Don't think that if you trigger this peak state of certainty and then you had a negative outcome to an encounter that you're still going to be in that sense of certainty. You're not. It'll dip. It's how, and it's nothing wrong with you. You're not weak. You're not inferior. You're not flawed. It's how human beings are built. And I will tell you, I always go back to that first day when I was out in the field training, selling meat and seafood door to door. And I was sitting beside the penguin who was my trainer that first day, right? And how I watched him go from a state of absolute certainty. And then after we got 20 doors slammed in his face, lose his state of certainty and start approaching his new door, each new door with like, uh, you know, his shoulders dropped, his head hung low. And then when he locked on the door, he had that sort of losers that very light knock. And then when the door opened, he'd be like, you wouldn't want any filet mignon, shrimp ball, lobster tails, right? I mean, the last 50 people said, no, so I'm assuming you're going to say no too. So just get it over with. Now, obviously he didn't say those words, but he didn't have to. He was oozing out of his pores, like toxic sludge. And that's what a negative state is like. Toxic sludge, toxic slime that hangs on you and drags you down. It brings you down. You're going to shake that shit off. Intra day, that's the key to state management. It's something that you do intra day. So today and every day going forward, I want you to focus on this ability to change your state in the middle of the day, in the moment. So either there's one of two things happening. You knock on a door, you pick up a phone, whatever it might be, you enter a sales account, right? And you have a positive outcome and you're like, yeah, and you feel great. In that case, you want to find your anchor and go into your higher state of certainty. You build on your success. Conversely, if it ends up going the wrong way, you don't close the Google, it's letting you face. I want you to take a deep breath, wait, and right before you're about to knock on the next door, bam, fire up your anchor and get back into a state of absolute certainty. Remember, the straight line system doesn't create certainty. It transfers certainty. The certainty has to come from within you. You get it? That white hot certainty that kind of just that so apparent that it's infectious. It sort of, it comes off of you, it's palpable, and it causes the other person to essentially, to buy by sheer force of energy exchange to essentially rise into a state of certainty. The certainty comes from you. The straight line system is the methodology, the system by which you can efficiently transfer certainty without breaking or violating any of the core rules of human influence that would essentially stop you from closing a sale. Like for instance, if you tried to transfer certainty, but you didn't know a way that alienated the client or broke rapport, you couldn't close. If you tried to transfer certainty to someone, but then you didn't focus on your energy and benefits outpad at the end, you wouldn't close. If you tried to transfer certainty, but you didn't focus on all three tensile, you didn't take the emotional picture as well, you couldn't close. So that's why when you combine state management with all the other overarching rules of the straight line system, you have a recipe to be able to close anyone who's close more and be awesome. That's the power of the straight line system.
